sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent acid reflux since childhood, which has worsened over the past few years. He currently takes 40 milligrams of omeprazole twice daily, and reports that it is not consistently effective. He identifies spicy foods, carbonated beverages, citrus products, and tomato-based foods as triggers, but does not eliminate them completely. He reports stress and anxiety as significant contributing factors. He has experienced a two-month period of decreased appetite. He notes that his weight has remained stable. He denies difficulty swallowing.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam is unremarkable. Abdominal surgical scars are noted. Heart and lungs are clear to auscultation. Weight is stable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a several-year history of poorly controlled gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). Stress and dietary triggers are contributing factors. An upper endoscopy is indicated to evaluate for potential causes of the GERD.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe nexium 40 milligrams twice daily. Schedule an upper endoscopy. Recommend dietary modifications to avoid identified trigger foods. Advise the patient to discuss stress management strategies. Follow up in 4 weeks to assess response to treatment and discuss endoscopy results.
